zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Hussey於1349年出生在英國薩默塞特時，她的父親Sir Edmund Hussey22歲，母親Lady Joanna Whitton19歲。她於1363年與John Whitton在英國薩默塞特結婚。他們至少是3個女兒的父母。她去世於1412年3月21日在英國薩默塞特法爾 格福德享年63歲，埋葬在英國薩默塞特法爾 格福德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Hussey于1349年出生在英国薩默塞特时，她的父亲Sir Edmund Hussey22岁，母亲Lady Joanna Whitton19岁。她于1363年与John Whitton在英国薩默塞特结婚。他们至少是3个女儿的父母。她去世于1412年3月21日在英国薩默塞特法爾 格福德享年63岁，埋葬在英国薩默塞特法爾 格福德。

nameMeanings.name: Hussey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a nickname for someone who habitually wore a distinctive pair of boots or gaiters, from Old French hosed, hoset, housé, Middle English hosey, hus(s)y,‘booted, gaitered’ (from Latin hosatus).

English: status name or nickname from Middle English hus(e)wyf ‘mistress of a family; wife of a householder’ (a compound of Old English hūs ‘house’ + wīf ‘woman’). Though originally a woman's name, it is often found as a male surname, presumably in a derogatory sense. The vocabulary word became hussie, with the meaning ‘disreputable woman’, in the 16th century; the surname, however, is not associated with this meaning.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hEodhusa ‘descendant of Eodhus’. This was the name of a bardic family associated with the Maguires of Fermanagh, also Anglicized as Oswell or Oswald .

variants.name: Hosea
meanings: English: variant of   Hussey  . Similarity with the Old Testament prophet's name Hosea (see 3 below) is accidental.
English: in Scotland, where the name is more common, it is sometimes a variant of   Hosie  , itself a form of   Hussey  , or else a variant of   Hossack  .
African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania): from the Biblical personal name Hosea. In the Old Testament, Hosea was a prophet who married a prostitute in order to illustrate Israel's unfaithfulness to God.

variants.name: Hosie
meanings: Scottish (northeast Scotland): variant of Irish   Hussey  .

variants.name: Oswell
meanings: Irish (Ulster): adopted as an English equivalent of Gaelic Ó hEodhusa ‘descendant of Eodhus’ (see Hussey ).

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Hussey was born in 1349, in Somerset, England, her father, Sir Edmund Hussey, was 22 and her mother, Lady Joanna Whitton, was 19. She married John Whitton in 1363, in Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 3 daughters. She died on 21 March 1412, in Farleigh Hungerford, Somerset, England, at the age of 63, and was buried in Farleigh Hungerford, Somerset, England.

Elle a épousé John Whitton en 1363, à Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 21 mars 1412, à Farleigh Hungerford, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterrée à Farleigh Hungerford, Somerset, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Husseyが1349年にイギリスソメルセトで生まれた時，父Sir Edmund Husseyは22歳，母Lady Joanna Whittonは19歳であった。1363年にイギリスソメルセトでJohn Whittonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1412年3月21日にイギリスソメルセトファルレイグ ハンジャーフォードで63歳で死去し，イギリスソメルセトファルレイグ ハンジャーフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Hussey(이)가 1349년 영국 서머세트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Edmund Hussey(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Joanna Whitton(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1363년 영국 서머세트에서 John Whitton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1412년 3월 21일 영국 서머세트 패를리 헌그르퍼드에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 서머세트 패를리 헌그르퍼드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Hussey родилась в 1349 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Edmund Hussey было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Joanna Whitton, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Whitton в 1363 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 дочери. Она умерла 21 марта 1412 г. в местности фарлиг хунгирфорд, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 63 лет и была похоронена в местности фарлиг хунгирфорд, сомирсет, Англия.
